experiment: Griffin (RecurrentGemma) vs Transformer ECG Anomaly Detection
hardware: Google Colab T4 GPU (15.6 GB VRAM)
key_findings: Griffin handles 8x longer sequences than Transformer before OOM (4096 vs 512)
Both models achieve similar accuracy (~95.7% vs ~95.8%) on ECG anomaly detection
Both models achieve similar F1 scores (~0.89)
Transformer has lower single-sample latency (2.2ms vs 72.3ms) at short sequences
Griffin memory scales linearly O(n) while Transformer scales quadratically O(n²)
For real-time ECG monitoring with long sequences, Griffin is the clear winner
